sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 18.58 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.18)

statement.latex: Let$G$be a group generated by finite number$n$of involutions in which$(uv)^4=1$for all involutions$u,v G$. Is it true that$G$is finite? is a 2-group? This is true for$n 3$. %What about$n=4$? Editors' comment of 2021: the previously published claim of an affirmative solution of this problem proved to be erroneous.

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory I_INFINITE_SEARCH; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
